post.content:
Hey everyone. Can anyone help me upsolve these problems?
**Q1**
	![image](https://assets.leetcode.com/users/images/f6c59bb0-cfd5-47a0-b97d-64fb67739283_1659069619.7420053.jpeg)
	![image](https://assets.leetcode.com/users/images/2eb2eab5-7c18-4c41-a624-088a1b259146_1659069658.390117.jpeg)
	
**Q2**
	![image](https://assets.leetcode.com/users/images/7d6b54da-9bb0-447c-a4a5-4aa16db36740_1659069785.3938956.jpeg)
	![image](https://assets.leetcode.com/users/images/106a92fd-f9fd-4c37-ab7d-9b2020ce85e0_1659069794.4549358.jpeg)
how many questions had to be done to get shortlisted ??

Q1, does "largest" mean "longest"? If yes, looking for the longest alternating subsequence [a, a+b, a, a+b,...] in the given seq. Not sure how, possibly count each number\'s presence between a\'s and find the most frequent. Note that b can be 0.\nQ2 probably dp, need 3 variables for state definition: current board index, remaining removals, cost of the last unremoved board. dp is the minimum cost to finish the the travel from current index to B with a given cost last unremoved and remaining removals. If current board removed, decrease remaining by 1, use last unremoved to calculate cost current to next. Otherwise, current board becomes the last unremoved. May TLE, any better idea?
